---
title: "Survey_descriptive_statistics"
output: html_notebook
---

This workflow describe the results from the surveys during the workshops in Belize during Oct 2024 

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
```

### Import data
```{r}
Fishery_survey <- read_excel("~/Documents/GitHub_projects/Networks_SSF_NatCap/data/processed/survey_answers.xlsx", sheet = "Fishers")
```

## Most common fishing gear used Lobster fishery

```{r}
# Filter data for Caye_Caulker
Caye_Caulker_data <- Fishery_survey %>%
  filter(Site == "Caye_Calker" & Specie == "Lobster")

# Filter data for Placencia
Placencia_data <- Fishery_survey %>%
  filter(Site != "Caye_Calker" & Specie == "Lobster")

# Filter data for all sites
All_data <- Fishery_survey %>%
  filter(Specie == "Lobster")

# Handle missing Fishing_gear_used values 
All_data$Fishing_gear_used[is.na(All_data$Fishing_gear_used)] <- "Unknown"
Caye_Caulker_data$Fishing_gear_used[is.na(Caye_Caulker_data$Fishing_gear_used)] <- "Unknown"
Placencia_data$Fishing_gear_used[is.na(Placencia_data$Fishing_gear_used)] <- "Unknown"

# Count occurrences of each category for Caye_Caulker
caye_counts <- Caye_Caulker_data %>%
  count(Fishing_gear_used, name = "Count")

# Count occurrences of each category for Placencia
Placencia_counts <- Placencia_data %>%
  count(Fishing_gear_used, name = "Count")

# Count occurences in total
All_counts <- All_data %>%
  count(Fishing_gear_used, name = "Count")

# Plot for Caye_Caulker
plot_caye <- ggplot(caye_counts, aes(x = Count, y = reorder(Fishing_gear_used, Count))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "grey") +
  labs(title = "Fishing Gears Used for Lobster in Caye Caulker", x = "Count", y = "Fishing Gear") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")

# Plot for Placencia
plot_Placencia <- ggplot(Placencia_counts, aes(x = Count, y = reorder(Fishing_gear_used, Count))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "grey") +
  labs(title = "Fishing Gears Used for Lobster in Placencia", x = "Count", y = "Fishing Gear") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")

# Plot for all
plot_all <- ggplot(All_counts, aes(x = Count, y = reorder(Fishing_gear_used, Count))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "grey") +
  labs(title = "Fishing Gears Used in the Lobster fishery", x = "Count", y = "Fishing Gear") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")

# Display plots
print(plot_caye)
print(plot_Placencia)
print(plot_all)

```

### Own their vessel and gear
```{r}
# Filter data for Lobster
Lobster_data <- Fishery_survey %>%
  filter(Specie == "Lobster")

# Check if the dataset is empty
if (nrow(Lobster_data) == 0) {
  stop("No data found for Specie == 'Lobster'. Verify the data.")
}

# Function to prepare pie chart data
prepare_pie_data <- function(data, site_name = "All Sites") {
  data %>%
    filter(Site == site_name | site_name == "All Sites") %>%
    group_by(Own_vessel_gear) %>%
    summarize(Count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(Percentage = Count / sum(Count) * 100)
}

# Prepare data for each site and all sites
caye_data <- prepare_pie_data(Lobster_data, "Caye_Calker")
placencia_data <- prepare_pie_data(Lobster_data, "Placencia")
all_sites_data <- prepare_pie_data(Lobster_data)

# Combine all datasets for plotting
pie_data <- bind_rows(caye_data, placencia_data, all_sites_data)

# Function to create a pie chart
create_pie_chart_with_percentages <- function(data, site_name) {
  ggplot(data, aes(x = "", y = Count, fill = Own_vessel_gear)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
    coord_polar("y", start = 0) +
    geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Percentage, 1), "%")), 
              position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 4, color = "white") +
    labs(title = paste("Ownership of Vessel and Gear:", site_name),
         fill = "Own Vessel & Gear",
         x = NULL, y = NULL) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(legend.position = "right")
}

# Create pie charts for each site
plot_caye <- create_pie_chart(caye_data, "Caye Caulker")
plot_placencia <- create_pie_chart(placencia_data, "Placencia")
plot_all_sites <- create_pie_chart(all_sites_data, "All Sites")

# Display the plots
print(plot_caye)
print(plot_placencia)
print(plot_all_sites)
```

### Number of fishing trips per month

```{r}
# 1. Box Plot: Distribution by Site
box_plot <- ggplot(Lobster_data, aes(x = Site, y = Fishing_trips_per_month_season, fill = Site)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Fishing Trips per Month by Site",
       x = "Site", y = "Fishing Trips per Month") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Display the plot
print(box_plot)

# Create the histogram
histogram_plot <- ggplot(Lobster_data, aes(x = Fishing_trips_per_month_season)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, color = "black", fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ Site) +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of Fishing Trips per Month by Site",
    x = "Fishing Trips per Month",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the histogram
print(histogram_plot)
```

### average number of fishing trips (multifishery participation)
```{r}
# Inspect and clean data
Fishery_survey <- Fishery_survey %>%
  mutate(Specie = str_trim(Specie), ID = str_trim(ID))

# Remove rows with NA values in Fishing_trips_per_month_season
Fishery_clean <- Fishery_survey %>%
  filter(!is.na(Fishing_trips_per_month_season), Specie %in% c("Lobster", "Conch", "Finfish"))

# Step 1: Summarize trips per fisher across fisheries
fisher_summary <- Fishery_clean %>%
  group_by(ID, Specie) %>%
  summarize(Total_Trips = sum(Fishing_trips_per_month_season), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Specie, values_from = Total_Trips, values_fill = 0)

# Step 2: Count active fisheries per fisher
fisher_summary <- fisher_summary %>%
  mutate(
    Active_Fisheries = rowSums(select(., Lobster, Conch, Finfish) > 0),
    Total_Days = Lobster + Conch + Finfish
  )

# Step 3: Calculate average days for one, two, or three fisheries
average_days <- fisher_summary %>%
  group_by(Active_Fisheries) %>%
  summarize(Average_Days = mean(Total_Days), .groups = "drop")

# Step 4: Count fishers in each category
fisher_counts <- fisher_summary %>%
  count(Active_Fisheries)

# Output Results
print("Average Number of Days by Active Fisheries:")
print(average_days)

print("Number of Fishers by Active Fisheries:")
print(fisher_counts)
```
